data.title: UR-Energy: Strengthening Cost Profile, Expanding Resources, and Contracted Sales Support Buy Rating and $2.50 Target
data.description:
Maxim Group analyst Tate Sullivan has maintained their bullish stance on URG stock, giving a Buy rating on March 12.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tate Sullivan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including UR-Energy’s improving cost profile and margin performance despite recent production headwinds at Lost Creek. He views the company’s ability to lower unit costs and generate better-than-expected gross margins, alongside a narrowing EBITDA loss and reduced cash burn, as evidence that operations are becoming more efficient and financially resilient.

In addition, Sullivan highlights the enhanced long-term outlook from the extended mine life and increased resources at Lost Creek, as well as the imminent startup of the Shirley Basin project, which should materially boost future output. He also points to UR-Energy’s growing contracted sales book, robust balance sheet after recent capital raises, and the supportive demand backdrop for uranium tied to expanding U.S. nuclear generation, all of which underpin his $2.50 price target and continued Buy recommendation.

In another report released on March 12, H.C. Wainwright also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $2.30 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
